description: Fix all font and color inconsistencies in the side panel UI to achieve pixel-perfect adherence to the retro design system. Ensures every text element uses the correct font family (font-head for titles/labels, font-sans for body, font-mono for code/technical values), standard Tailwind text sizes (no arbitrary pixel values), and theme color tokens (no hardcoded colors).

userStories.title: Fix ToolRow display name arbitrary text size
userStories.description: As a designer, I want tool display names to use a standard Tailwind text size so that the type scale is consistent across all UI elements.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolRow display name uses `text-sm` (14px) instead of `text-[13px]`
Tool names visually align with plugin display names which also use `text-sm`
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx, line 29. Change `text-[13px]` to `text-sm` in the className of the div wrapping `{displayName}`. The current 13px is an arbitrary value between text-xs (12px) and text-sm (14px) that doesn't align with any other text in the UI. Plugin display names in PluginCard.tsx and BrowserToolsCard.tsx use `text-sm`, so tool names should match.

userStories.title: Fix error alert arbitrary text size in PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard
userStories.description: As a designer, I want inline error alerts to use a standard text size so that the type scale has no arbitrary pixel values.
acceptanceCriteria: Error alerts in PluginCard use `text-xs` instead of `text-[11px]`
Error alerts in BrowserToolsCard use `text-xs` instead of `text-[11px]`
userStories.notes:
Two files to edit:
1. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx — lines 163 and 168, both have `text-[11px]` in the Alert className. Change to `text-xs`.
2. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx — line 146, same pattern. Change `text-[11px]` to `text-xs`.

11px is an arbitrary value between text-[10px] (used for badges) and text-xs (12px). These inline error messages should match the standard small text size used elsewhere (tool count labels, descriptions, etc.).

userStories.title: Add font-head to FailedPluginCard title for card heading consistency
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the FailedPluginCard title to use font-head like every other card heading so that the typography is consistent across all card types.
acceptanceCriteria: FailedPluginCard 'Failed to load' title has `font-head` class
Title visually matches the heading weight/style of PluginCard, BrowserToolsCard, and NpmPluginCard headings
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/FailedPluginCard.tsx, line 12. The span currently has `text-destructive text-sm font-medium`. Add `font-head` to the className. Every other card title in the UI uses font-head:
- PluginCard name: `font-head text-sm` (PluginCard.tsx:116)
- BrowserToolsCard name: `font-head text-sm` (BrowserToolsCard.tsx:125)
- NpmPluginCard name: `font-head text-sm` (NpmPluginCard.tsx:26)
- Empty state titles: use `<Text as="h3">` which resolves to `font-head` (Empty.tsx:34)
- ConfirmationDialog title: uses `<Text as="h6">` which resolves to `font-head`

The FailedPluginCard is the only card title missing `font-head`. Also remove `font-medium` since font-head (Archivo Black) is already bold weight and font-medium is redundant/conflicting.

userStories.title: Change NpmPluginCard author from font-mono to font-sans
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the plugin author name to use the body font (font-sans) instead of the code font (font-mono) so that font-mono is reserved for technical values like version numbers, port numbers, and CLI commands.
acceptanceCriteria: NpmPluginCard author span uses `font-sans` instead of `font-mono`
font-mono in the side panel is used only for: port numbers, version strings, CLI commands, tool parameter previews, countdown timers, and code blocks
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/NpmPluginCard.tsx, line 36. Change `font-mono` to `font-sans` in the className of the author span. The author name (e.g., 'opentabs-dev') is metadata — not code. font-mono is used throughout the UI for technical values: port numbers (Footer.tsx:34), version strings (ConfirmationDialog.tsx:50), CLI commands (EmptyStates.tsx:42,58,76), tool names (ConfirmationDialog.tsx:110), parameter previews (ConfirmationDialog.tsx:125). Author names don't belong in this category.

userStories.title: Remove redundant font-head from accordion trigger wrappers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove redundant font-head declarations from accordion trigger wrappers so that the font family is set in exactly one place per text element.
acceptanceCriteria: AccordionPrimitive.Trigger in PluginCard no longer has `font-head` — only the inner name div has it
AccordionPrimitive.Trigger in BrowserToolsCard no longer has `font-head` — only the inner name div has it
Plugin names and Browser title still render in Archivo Black (font-head)
userStories.notes:
Two files to edit:
1. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx, line 96 — remove `font-head` from the AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className. The inner name div at line 116 already has `font-head text-foreground`.
2. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx, line 117 — same pattern: remove `font-head` from the AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className. The inner name div at line 125 already has `font-head text-foreground`.

Currently `font-head` is on both the outer trigger (which wraps icon + name + chevron) and the inner name div. The inner one wins for the name text, but the outer one unnecessarily applies Archivo Black to the chevron icon area. Removing it from the outer wrapper is cleaner — each piece of text declares its own font.

userStories.title: Remove redundant text-primary-foreground from Tooltip base class
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Tooltip component's base CVA class to not set a text color, since every variant already specifies its own text color, eliminating the redundant declaration.
acceptanceCriteria: `text-primary-foreground` is removed from the base string of `tooltipContentVariants` in Tooltip.tsx
All three tooltip variants (default, primary, solid) still render with correct text colors
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Tooltip.tsx, line 8. The base CVA string is:
`'z-50 overflow-hidden rounded border-2 border-border bg-background px-3 py-1.5 text-xs text-primary-foreground animate-in ...'`

Remove `text-primary-foreground` from this base string. Each variant already sets its own text color:
- default: `bg-background text-foreground`
- primary: `bg-primary text-primary-foreground`
- solid: `bg-foreground text-background`

The base `text-primary-foreground` (black) is immediately overridden by the default variant's `text-foreground`. It's redundant and could cause confusion if a new variant forgets to set text color (it would silently inherit black from the base). Also remove the redundant `bg-background` from the base string for the same reason — each variant sets its own background.
